name.text: Finding the coordinates of the triangle vertices after the enlargement

questiontext.text:
The vertices of triangle \(\triangle ABC\) are A({@x1@}, {@y1@}), B({@x2@}, {@y2@}), and C({@x3@}, {@y3@}). The triangle is enlarged with a centre of enlargement at (0, 0) and a scale factor of {@sf@}.

[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="400px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 10, 10, -10], axis: true, showCopyright: false}); // Original Shape var poly1 = board.create('polygon', [{#pA#}, {#pB#}, {#pC#}], {fillColor: 'blue', fillOpacity: 0.3, name: 'Original'}); // Lines of projection through the origin to help visualize negative SF board.create('line', [[0,0], {#pA#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', fixed: true}); board.create('line', [[0,0], {#pB#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', fixed: true}); board.create('line', [[0,0], {#pC#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', fixed: true}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Find the coordinates of the image vertices after the enlargement. Write your answers in the form [x,y].

  • A' = [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]
  • B' = [[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]] [[feedback:prt2]]
  • C' = [[input:ans3]] [[validation:ans3]] [[feedback:prt3]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

Step 1: Apply the Enlargement Rule

When the centre of enlargement is the origin \((0,0)\), we multiply each coordinate by the scale factor \(k\):

\[ (x, y) \rightarrow (k \cdot x, k \cdot y) \]

Step 2: Calculate the New Coordinates

Given the scale factor \(k = {@sf@}\):

  • A({@x1@}, {@y1@}) \(\rightarrow\) ({@sf@} \(\cdot\) {@x1@}, {@sf@} \(\cdot\) {@y1@}) = {@pA_prime@}
  • B({@x2@}, {@y2@}) \(\rightarrow\) ({@sf@} \(\cdot\) {@x2@}, {@sf@} \(\cdot\) {@y2@}) = {@pB_prime@}
  • C({@x3@}, {@y3@}) \(\rightarrow\) ({@sf@} \(\cdot\) {@x3@}, {@sf@} \(\cdot\) {@y3@}) = {@pC_prime@}
[[jsxgraph width="450px" height="450px"]] var board2 = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-15, 15, 15, -15], axis: true, showCopyright: false}); // Original Triangle (Blue) var poly_orig = board2.create('polygon', [{#pA#}, {#pB#}, {#pC#}], {fillColor: 'blue', fillOpacity: 0.2, strokeColor: 'blue', name: 'Original', withLabel: true}); // Enlarged Image (Red, Inverted, Opposite Side) var poly_image = board2.create('polygon', [{#pA_prime#}, {#pB_prime#}, {#pC_prime#}], {fillColor: 'red', fillOpacity: 0.3, strokeColor: 'red', name: 'Image', withLabel: true}); // Lines passing through origin to show negative SF board2.create('line', [[0,0], {#pA#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', fixed: true}); board2.create('line', [[0,0], {#pB#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', fixed: true}); board2.create('line', [[0,0], {#pC#}], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', fixed: true}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Because the scale factor is negative, the image appears on the opposite side of the centre of enlargement and is inverted compared to the original shape.

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
